title: bOpen plugin setup manifest
description: Declares a plugin's installable dependencies (CLIs, env keys, third-party skills, agent delivery, hooks, and skill setup scripts) for the bopen-tools setup installer. Manifests are declarative only — no install state is ever recorded here. One manifest lives at <plugin-root>/setup/manifest.json.

plugin.description: The plugin name, matching its entry in the marketplace catalog and .claude-plugin/plugin.json (e.g. "bopen-tools").

clis.description: Command-line tools this plugin's skills shell out to. Each entry is checked live by the detector; nothing here reflects current install state.

env.description: Environment variables this plugin's skills read. The detector only checks presence/non-emptiness of the value — it never reads or displays the value itself.

thirdPartySkills.description: Skills installed via `npx skills add` (or similar) from an external source rather than shipped inside this plugin.

agents.description: How this plugin delivers agent definitions per runtime.

hooks.description: Pointer to this plugin's hook catalog and its per-user/per-project enable/disable config. Omit if the plugin ships no hooks.

skillSetupScripts.description: Per-skill setup.sh-style scripts that provision credentials or local install state (e.g. an API token) beyond what the CLI/env/third-party-skill checks cover.

check.description: How the detector evaluates whether this dependency is satisfied. Three forms: a plain shell command (exit 0 = installed), `path:<glob>` (a `~`-expanded glob that must match at least one file to count as installed), or `env:<KEY>` (the named environment variable must be set and non-empty).

usedBy.description: Names of skills (or a free-text note) that depend on this CLI, so the UI can show what breaks without it.
Names of skills that depend on this env key.

obtain.description: Human-readable instructions for where to get a value for this key (a URL and/or navigation path, or a setup script to run).

name.description: The skill's directory/name as installed under ~/.claude/skills/.

source.description: The GitHub org/repo (or equivalent) the skill was installed from, e.g. "vercel-labs/agent-skills".

install.description: The exact command that installs this skill, e.g. an `npx skills add` invocation.

agentDelivery.description: How agent definitions for a single runtime are delivered: either bundled directly inside the installed plugin (no extra step needed), or via a setup script the detector can verify with a check.

oneOf.description: Agent .md files ship inside the plugin and are auto-discovered by the runtime; no separate install step exists.

script.description: Path (relative to the plugin root) to the script that delivers agent definitions to this runtime.

manifest.description: Path (relative to the plugin root) to this plugin's hook catalog, e.g. "hooks/manifest.json".

config.description: Path (may use `~`) to the per-user hooks enable/disable config file, e.g. "~/.claude/bopen-tools/hooks-config.json". Writing this file is ask-tier — the installer never writes it itself.

skill.description: The skill this setup script belongs to.

script.description: Path (relative to the plugin root) to the setup script.

purpose.description: One-line human-readable description of what the script provisions.

installCommands.description: Platform-specific install commands. At least one of `any`, `darwin`, or `linux` must be present. `any` is used when the command is identical across platforms; platform-specific keys take precedence over `any` when both are present for the current platform.

any.description: Install command to use regardless of platform.

darwin.description: Install command to use on macOS.

linux.description: Install command to use on Linux.
